nodes.label: ALPHA_FINDINGS.md (model does not beat the market)
nodes.norm_label: alpha_findings.md (model does not beat the market)

nodes.label: has_odds Flag
nodes.rationale: Added 2026-06-09 because rows without odds carry a 0.5/0.5 implied-prob sentinel fill that passed dropna and produced phantom 2.00 odds in the backtest; the perspective-symmetric flag lets the model and downstream consumers distinguish real market information from the fill without re-deriving from raw odds columns. Only ~32% of historical rows (~65% recent) have real odds.

nodes.label: Perspective-Neutral Inference Rule
nodes.rationale: Permanent rule: any inference outside train.py MUST replicate the training preprocessing (train medians, then StandardScaler) and randomize the row perspective (swap w_/l_ pairs, negate diff_/rank_diff/age_diff/height_diff, invert rank_ratio, 1-p for elo probs). Even with correct preprocessing, non-randomized winner-POV rows show +11pt orientation bias (76% vs 65%).

nodes.label: Fake 85% Win-Rate Artifact (4 compounding bugs)
nodes.rationale: The old src/models/backtest.py reported 85% win rate and an exploding bankroll via four compounding bugs: (1) no scaler/train medians — raw inputs degenerate the model to 95.6% 'p1 wins' predictions; (2) winner-POV rows never randomized — betting p1 auto-wins by construction; (3) the 0.5/0.5 implied-prob sentinel passed dropna, creating phantom 2.00 odds; (4) de-vigged fair odds used instead of real B365 prices with margin.

nodes.label: Closing Line Value (CLV) as the Real Edge Metric
nodes.rationale: Win/loss on single bets is noise; a signal is only 'serious' if the price taken beats the closing sharp line over time. CLV is the provable edge metric — sell nothing until CLV is positive and stable. Adopted 2026-06-08 as the strategy direction (CLV + spread/handicap, arbitrage dropped).
nodes.norm_label: closing line value (clv) as the real edge metric

nodes.label: Sharp No-Vig Consensus as Fair Line
nodes.rationale: The ML model does NOT beat the market (ALPHA_FINDINGS), so the reliable 'prediction' is the no-vig consensus of sharp books (Pinnacle + commission-adjusted exchanges). A value bet is a SOFT book offering a price above that fair line — a single +EV wager, not arbitrage. max_edge=0.20 caps stale/error lines because huge edges are traps; loose books (onexbet) are excluded.
nodes.norm_label: sharp no-vig consensus as fair line

nodes.label: Temporal Leakage via Pre-Split Imputation (C1)
nodes.rationale: fillna(median) computed on the whole dataset before the temporal split contaminated training with the 2025+ distribution, inflating accuracy to 78.8%. Fix: compute medians on train only, save atp_medians.pkl, reuse at inference; per-fold medians for walk-forward CV. Fixed 2026-05-21; honest accuracy dropped to 66-67%.

nodes.label: Honest Baseline: 66.3% acc / LL 0.608 vs B365 favourite 67.7%
nodes.rationale: Leak-free 2025+ test baseline (train 2005-2023, val 2024): ensemble 66.3% acc / LL 0.608 / ROC 0.731; LightGBM single best 67.0%. The B365 favourite scores 67.7% — the market is still more accurate than the model, hence no predictive edge; honest Quarter-Kelly backtest shows ROI -82%, win rate 43%. This is the baseline every experiment must beat.
nodes.norm_label: honest baseline: 66.3% acc / ll 0.608 vs b365 favourite 67.7%

nodes.label: Style ELO (vs-server / vs-returner)
nodes.rationale: Contextual ELO tracking how good a player is at beating specific opponent archetypes: ratings update only when the opponent qualifies as a big server (ace rate > 8% over 200+ serve points) or elite returner (return points won > 38% over 200+ return points), classified dynamically from accumulated historical stats. Listed as a core kept feature; P4 item marked COMPLETATO.

nodes.label: Fractional (Quarter) Kelly Staking with Caps
nodes.rationale: Quarter Kelly (0.25 fraction) with a 2% bankroll stake cap and min odds 1.30 (super-favourites are a Kelly variance trap). Kelly compounding inflates backtest profit massively but is unrealistic: bookmaker limits, line movement from large bets, model degradation, and execution slippage — so flat staking is the reliable measure of model quality.

nodes.label: Self-Evolving Experiment Loop
nodes.rationale: Weekly job picks the TOP unchecked experiment, implements the smallest viable version, evaluates honestly (temporal test 2025+, perspective-randomized, train medians + scaler), records the result, keeps the change only if it beats the baseline, reverts and marks FAILED otherwise. After 3 consecutive failures: stop and write findings for the human. NEVER pushes to remote, NEVER touches live betting code (src/betting/) without a human.

nodes.label: E1 — Serve-Stats Coverage 2025-26 (biggest accuracy lever)
nodes.rationale: _50 rolling serve/return features are 84% NaN on the 2025-26 test years because Sackmann data lags the current season — the model flies blind (elo + odds + form only) exactly where it predicts. Ingesting current-season match stats is expected to be the single biggest gain, with a legit ceiling around ROC 0.80 / 70-72% accuracy. The 2026-06-09 split-refresh experiment confirmed split changes alone don't move accuracy; data coverage is the lever.

nodes.label: Winner-POV Row Leakage
nodes.rationale: Feature rows are stored winner-POV (target=1 always; w_ side is the actual winner). Any consumer that does not flip ~50% of rows leaks the label: betting on p1 auto-wins. w_/l_ feature pairs must stay paired (the 2026-05-22 unpaired-columns leak inflated acc to 77.8% / ROI +46%, fixed by _enforce_perspective_pairs) and diff_ features carry winner-tilt sign pre-flip. Honest feature tilt is ~0.55-0.66; >0.75 is a leak suspect.

nodes.label: Flat Staking as True-Edge Baseline
nodes.rationale: Flat EUR 10 staking avoids Kelly compounding distortion and shows the TRUE edge; flat ROI is the reliable measure of model quality. Realism layered on top: stochastic slippage 0-2% worse odds and 5% commission on net winnings (implements the P1 realism recommendations). Kelly-on-growing-bankroll results are mathematically valid but unrealistic in practice.

nodes.label: Market Implied Probability Feature (de-vigged B365→PS→Avg)
nodes.rationale: Bookmaker implied probability with the overround removed (B365 primary, Pinnacle secondary, Avg fallback) acts as a powerful SOTA baseline feature, transforming the model into a differential error-corrector on top of the market. Missing values are filled with the 0.5/0.5 coin-flip sentinel to keep historical rows — which is exactly why the has_odds flag exists.

nodes.label: config.py — Dashboard Constants (127.0.0.1:8765, paths, shells)
nodes.rationale: Local-only by design: HOST hardcoded to 127.0.0.1, comment says 'never bind 0.0.0.0'. Optional DASHBOARD_TOKEN read dynamically so tests can monkeypatch the env.

nodes.label: COMMAND_WHITELIST — UI Pipeline Command Map
nodes.rationale: Only these 7 commands (download/clean/features/train/backtest/inference/signals) can be launched from UI buttons; free-form execution is deliberately routed through the real terminals instead of this map.

nodes.label: server.py — FastAPI App Assembly (Tennis Mission Control)
nodes.rationale: docs_url/redoc_url disabled; app = static frontend + REST data API + WS runner/terminals composed via include_router.
nodes.norm_label: server.py — fastapi app assembly (tennis mission control)

nodes.label: data_api.py — Read-Only REST Cockpit (/api)
nodes.rationale: Read-only data cockpit: sqlite opened with file:...?mode=ro URI so the dashboard can never corrupt betanalytix.db. The config editor PUT is explicitly 'the only writing endpoint'.

nodes.label: app.js — Mission Control Frontend (vanilla JS, no build step)
nodes.rationale: Deliberately buildless vanilla JS: lazy tab loading via `loaded` map, hand-rolled canvas CLV chart, xterm.js Terminal+FitAddon per terminal tab, single shared /ws/run socket for pipeline buttons.
nodes.norm_label: app.js — mission control frontend (vanilla js, no build step)

nodes.label: index.html — Mission Control Shell (8 tabs, xterm.js CDN)
nodes.rationale: Tabs: Overview/Segnali/Bet/CLV/Quote/Pipeline/Terminali/Config. xterm@5.3.0 and xterm-addon-fit@0.8.0 loaded from jsdelivr CDN — the only external runtime dependencies of the frontend.
nodes.norm_label: index.html — mission control shell (8 tabs, xterm.js cdn)

nodes.label: test_dashboard_api.py — Dashboard API Tests (temp DB + whitelist reject)
nodes.rationale: Implements the spec checklist: REST endpoints return valid JSON against a monkeypatched temp sqlite DB; /ws/run rejects 'rm -rf /'; PUT /api/config rejects invalid YAML with 400.
nodes.norm_label: test_dashboard_api.py — dashboard api tests (temp db + whitelist reject)

nodes.label: smoke_dashboard_ws.py — Live WS Smoke Test (terminal + runner)
nodes.rationale: Proof-of-execution trick: sends Write-Output ('MC_'+'TERM_OK') so the typed command never contains the literal marker — seeing MC_TERM_OK in ANSI-stripped output proves the shell actually executed it, not just echoed it.
nodes.norm_label: smoke_dashboard_ws.py — live ws smoke test (terminal + runner)

nodes.label: inspect_betanalytix.py — betanalytix.db Schema Dump (one-off)
nodes.rationale: One-off helper written to dump betanalytix.db schema + row counts specifically 'for dashboard build' — informed which tables/columns the REST cockpit queries.

nodes.label: run_loop.ps1 — Headless Claude Loop Runner (Task Scheduler)
nodes.rationale: Invoked by Windows Task Scheduler; runs claude.cmd -p with --permission-mode bypassPermissions, prompt = 'read and execute scripts/loops/<Loop>.md autonomously'; stdout/stderr redirected to reports/loops/<loop>_<stamp>.log; logs older than 30 days auto-deleted; exits with claude's exit code.
nodes.norm_label: run_loop.ps1 — headless claude loop runner (task scheduler)

nodes.label: nightly_maintenance.md — Nightly Loop Prompt (data+retrain+metrics)
nodes.rationale: Conservative by rule: never push to remote, never call paid APIs, never modify the EXPERIMENTS.md queue (only the weekly loop may), ~30 min budget. Sole EXPERIMENTS.md exception: write a regression alert if accuracy drops >1pt or log_loss rises >0.01 between the last two metrics_history.csv rows.

nodes.label: weekly_evolution.md — Weekly Evolution Loop Prompt (one experiment/run)
nodes.rationale: One experiment per run, smallest possible implementation, ~60 min budget. Honest-eval only (test 2025+, randomized perspective, train medians + scaler). Keep if acc +0.3pt or LL -0.005 without worsening the other, else git revert. Circuit breaker: 3 consecutive FAILED in Done → write 'LOOP FERMO — servono decisioni umane' and stop. Feature changes must pass probe_feature_tilt (no feature >0.70 tilt).
nodes.norm_label: weekly_evolution.md — weekly evolution loop prompt (one experiment/run)

nodes.label: Mission Control — Local Web Cockpit (FastAPI, 127.0.0.1:8765)
nodes.rationale: Hermes-style local personal cockpit, deliberately NOT a hardened web app: localhost-only binding, optional token, two-tier execution model (whitelisted pipeline buttons for safety + full PTY terminals for power, arbitrary code execution accepted by design), read-only DB access, YAML editor as the single write path with backup.

nodes.label: Real PTY Terminal Bridge (pywinpty ConPTY ↔ WebSocket ↔ xterm.js)
nodes.rationale: Pattern: server spawns a real Windows ConPTY per WS connection (PowerShell or WSL), pumps raw bytes to the browser where xterm.js renders them; input/resize travel as JSON frames the other way. Multiple connections = multiple independent terminals.
nodes.norm_label: real pty terminal bridge (pywinpty conpty ↔ websocket ↔ xterm.js)

nodes.label: Windows Scheduled Tasks — TennisLoopNightly (07:13) / TennisLoopWeekly (Sun 09:23)
nodes.rationale: Self-evolution made unattended: Task Scheduler fires run_loop.ps1 which runs claude -p headless; nightly = data+retrain+metrics maintenance, weekly = one experiment from EXPERIMENTS.md.
